text: This study aimed to link philosophical ethics and the prophetic values in Islamic education in order to construct a humanistic, balanced and transformative pedagogy. Philosophical ethics are a product of the human intellect and the social structure, while the prophetic values are a divine product which is based on the teachings of the Holy Quran and the Hadith. The method used in this research is qualitative library research with content analysis. The results of this study demonstrate that the linking of philosophical ethics and the prophetic values leads to the establishment of a new comprehensive approach in education which can be defined as humanistic, balanced and transformative. In this respect, humanistic education preserves human honor and capacity, balanced education maintains justice in the process of education and its means and transformative education cultivates the critical skills of the student so that he or she can change the society meaningfully. Hence, linking the philosophical ethics and the prophetic values in Islamic education develops a high-level educational structure in which the student can achieve success both academically and morally through the adoption of Islamic ethics. Based on the findings of this study, the proposed educational structure is necessary for solving the universal human problems which require the cultivation of the student's physical, intellectual and spiritual aspects.
